It has been used in traditional medicine for its health benefits, which include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Aceras anthora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aceras anthora is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusions, often combined with other herbs to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
